---
title: "Fraud Analysis Dashboard"
subtitle: "Quarto starter with sample visuals and an embedded AI assistant"
format: dashboard
resources:
  - scripts/chat.js
  - styles/dashboard.css
  - data/*.csv
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from IPython.display import HTML

open_payments = pd.read_csv("data/open_payments.csv")
suppliers = pd.read_csv("data/suppliers.csv")
surges_count = pd.read_csv("data/surges_count.csv")
supplier_spike_trend = pd.read_csv("data/supplier_spike_trend.csv")
supplier_hcpcs_table = pd.read_csv("data/supplier_hcPCS_table.csv")
surges_products = pd.read_csv("data/surges_products.csv")
referring_providers = pd.read_csv("data/referring_providers.csv")
risk_signals = pd.read_csv("data/risk_signals.csv")

def style_plot(fig):
    fig.update_layout(
        paper_bgcolor="#ffffff",
        plot_bgcolor="#f0fdf4",
        font=dict(color="#14532d"),
        margin=dict(l=30, r=20, t=60, b=30),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    )
    fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=False, zeroline=False)
    fig.update_yaxes(gridcolor="rgba(20, 83, 45, 0.12)", zeroline=False)
    return fig
```

# Open Payments

## Row {height="34%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Open Payments Snapshot"

open_payment_total = open_payments["amount_m"].sum()
open_payment_records = open_payments["payment_count"].sum()
open_payment_risk = open_payments["risk_index"].mean()

HTML(f"""
<div class='metric-grid'>
  <div class='metric-tile'>
    <div class='metric-label'>Total Payments</div>
    <div class='metric-value'>${open_payment_total:,.1f}M</div>
    <div class='metric-sub'>Sample modeled amount</div>
  </div>
  <div class='metric-tile'>
    <div class='metric-label'>Payment Records</div>
    <div class='metric-value'>{open_payment_records:,.0f}</div>
    <div class='metric-sub'>Across four regions</div>
  </div>
  <div class='metric-tile'>
    <div class='metric-label'>Avg. Risk Index</div>
    <div class='metric-value'>{open_payment_risk:.1f}</div>
    <div class='metric-sub'>Scaled 0 to 100</div>
  </div>
</div>
""")
```

## Row {height="66%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Payments by Category"

payments_by_category = (
    open_payments
    .groupby("payment_category", as_index=False)["amount_m"]
    .sum()
    .sort_values("amount_m", ascending=False)
)
fig = px.bar(
    payments_by_category,
    x="payment_category",
    y="amount_m",
    text_auto=".1f",
    labels={"payment_category": "Category", "amount_m": "Amount (Millions)"},
)
fig = style_plot(fig)
fig.show()
```

```{python}
#| title: "Regional Share of Open Payments"

payments_by_region = (
    open_payments
    .groupby("region", as_index=False)["amount_m"]
    .sum()
    .sort_values("amount_m", ascending=False)
)
fig = px.pie(
    payments_by_region,
    names="region",
    values="amount_m",
    hole=0.45,
)
fig = style_plot(fig)
fig.show()
```

# Suppliers

## Row {height="34%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Supplier Snapshot"

supplier_spend_total = surges_count["Total_Spend"].sum()
supplier_risk_orders = surges_count["Surge_Supplier_Count"].sum()

mini_fig = px.line(
    supplier_spike_trend,
    x="year",
    y="tot_suplr_srvcs",
    markers=True
)

mini_fig.update_layout(
    height=180,
    margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
    xaxis_title=None,
    yaxis_title=None,
    showlegend=False
)

mini_fig.update_traces(line=dict(width=3))

mini_chart_html = mini_fig.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs=False)

HTML(f"""
<div class='metric-grid'>

  <div class='metric-tile'>
    <div class='metric-label'>Total Supplier Spend (2021–2023)</div>
    <div class='metric-value'>${supplier_spend_total/1000000:,.0f}M</div>
    <div class='metric-sub'>Across strategic vendors</div>
  </div>

  <div class='metric-tile'>
    <div class='metric-label'>High-Risk Orders</div>
    <div class='metric-value'>{supplier_risk_orders:,.0f}</div>
    <div class='metric-sub'>Flagged in sample history</div>
  </div>

  <div class='metric-tile'>
    <div class='metric-label'>Supplier Activity Trend</div>
    
    <!-- 🔥 YOUR SPIKE LINE LIVES HERE -->
    <div>{mini_chart_html}</div>
    
    <div class='metric-sub'>Spike & disappearance pattern</div>
  </div>

</div>
""")

```



```{python}
#| title: "Supplier HCPCS Billing"

supplier_hcpcs_table

```
## Row {height="66%"}
```{python}
#| title: "Surges suppliers and sold products"

surges_products = surges_products.drop(surges_products.columns[0], axis=1)
surges_products

```

# Referring Providers

## Row {height="34%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Provider Snapshot"

provider_claims_total = referring_providers["referred_claims"].sum()
provider_flagged_total = referring_providers["flagged_referrals"].sum()
provider_avg_risk = referring_providers["provider_risk_score"].mean()

HTML(f"""
<div class='metric-grid'>
  <div class='metric-tile'>
    <div class='metric-label'>Referred Claims</div>
    <div class='metric-value'>{provider_claims_total:,.0f}</div>
    <div class='metric-sub'>Across high-volume referrers</div>
  </div>
  <div class='metric-tile'>
    <div class='metric-label'>Flagged Referrals</div>
    <div class='metric-value'>{provider_flagged_total:,.0f}</div>
    <div class='metric-sub'>Triggered review rules</div>
  </div>
  <div class='metric-tile'>
    <div class='metric-label'>Avg. Provider Risk</div>
    <div class='metric-value'>{provider_avg_risk:.1f}</div>
    <div class='metric-sub'>Scaled 0 to 100</div>
  </div>
</div>
""")
```


## Row {height="66%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Referrals by Specialty"

claims_by_specialty = (
    referring_providers
    .groupby("specialty", as_index=False)["referred_claims"]
    .sum()
    .sort_values("referred_claims", ascending=False)
)
fig = px.bar(
    claims_by_specialty,
    x="specialty",
    y="referred_claims",
    text_auto=True,
    labels={"specialty": "Specialty", "referred_claims": "Referred Claims"},
)
fig = style_plot(fig)
fig.show()
```

```{python}
#| title: "Flagged Referrals by State"

flagged_by_state = (
    referring_providers
    .groupby("state", as_index=False)["flagged_referrals"]
    .sum()
    .sort_values("flagged_referrals", ascending=False)
)
fig = px.pie(
    flagged_by_state,
    names="state",
    values="flagged_referrals",
    hole=0.45,
)
fig = style_plot(fig)
fig.show()
```

# Risk Signals

## Row {height="34%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Risk Signal Snapshot"

risk_cases_total = risk_signals["open_cases"].sum()
risk_exposure_total = risk_signals["exposure_m"].sum()
risk_confidence_avg = risk_signals["rule_confidence"].mean()

HTML(f"""
<div class='metric-grid'>
  <div class='metric-tile'>
    <div class='metric-label'>Open Cases</div>
    <div class='metric-value'>{risk_cases_total:,.0f}</div>
    <div class='metric-sub'>Across active signals</div>
  </div>
  <div class='metric-tile'>
    <div class='metric-label'>Exposure</div>
    <div class='metric-value'>${risk_exposure_total:,.1f}M</div>
    <div class='metric-sub'>Estimated recoverable amount</div>
  </div>
  <div class='metric-tile'>
    <div class='metric-label'>Avg. Rule Confidence</div>
    <div class='metric-value'>{risk_confidence_avg:.1f}</div>
    <div class='metric-sub'>Scaled 0 to 100</div>
  </div>
</div>
""")
```

## Row {height="66%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Open Cases by Signal Type"

signal_cases = risk_signals.sort_values("open_cases", ascending=False)
fig = px.bar(
    signal_cases,
    x="signal_type",
    y="open_cases",
    color="severity_band",
    text_auto=True,
    labels={"signal_type": "Signal Type", "open_cases": "Open Cases"},
)
fig = style_plot(fig)
fig.show()
```

```{python}
#| title: "Exposure by Severity Band"

exposure_by_severity = (
    risk_signals
    .groupby("severity_band", as_index=False)["exposure_m"]
    .sum()
    .sort_values("exposure_m", ascending=False)
)
fig = px.pie(
    exposure_by_severity,
    names="severity_band",
    values="exposure_m",
    hole=0.45,
)
fig = style_plot(fig)
fig.show()
```

# AI Assistant

## Row {height="270px"}
```{python}
#| title: "How to Use & Limitations"

HTML("""
<style>
.card-body {
  height: fit-content !important;
  padding-bottom: 8px !important;
}
.info-panel {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 12px;
  padding: 8px;
  height: fit-content;
}
.info-box {
  flex: 1;
  align-self: flex-start;
  background: #f0fdf4;
  border-radius: 10px;
  padding: clamp(8px, 1.5vw, 20px);
  font-size: clamp(12px, 1.2vw, 16px);
  color: #14532d;
  border-left: 3px solid;
}
.info-box.instructions { border-color: #38bdf8; }
.info-box.limitations  { border-color: #f87171; }
.info-box h4 {
  margin: 0 0 clamp(4px, 0.8vw, 10px) 0;
  font-size: clamp(12px, 1.2vw, 16px);
  font-weight: 700;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.05em;
}
.info-box.instructions h4 { color: #38bdf8; }
.info-box.limitations  h4 { color: #f87171; }
.info-box ol {
  margin: 0;
  padding-left: clamp(12px, 1.5vw, 20px);
  line-height: clamp(1.6, 1.8vw, 1.8);
  color: #94a3b8;
}
@media (max-width: 768px) {
  .info-panel {
    flex-direction: column;
  }
}
</style>

<div class="info-panel">
  <div class="info-box instructions">
    <h4>How to Use</h4>
    <ol>
      <li>Type a person's name, business name, or topic into the chat box and press <strong>Send</strong> or hit <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
      <li>The AI will search the relevant database and return a <strong>Summary</strong> and <strong>Fraud Analysis</strong>.</li>
      <li>For best results, use a full name, business name, or NPI number.</li>
      <li>To target a specific database, mention <strong>OIG</strong> or <strong>SOS</strong> in your question.</li>
    </ol>
  </div>
  <div class="info-box limitations">
    <h4>Limitations</h4>
    <ol>
      <li>Information is sourced from two databases only — <strong>HHS OIG</strong> and <strong>Missouri Secretary of State (SOS)</strong>.</li>
      <li>The AI searches <strong>one database per question</strong>. If none is specified, it will decide based on your question.</li>
      <li>Responses are based on the <strong>first result returned</strong>. More results can be included but will increase response time.</li>
      <li>The AI is limited to <strong>2 questions per minute</strong>. If you receive an error, wait a moment and try again.</li>
    </ol>
  </div>
</div>
""")
```

## Row {height="70%"}
```{python}
#| title: "AI Fraud Assistant"

HTML("""
<style>
.chat-shell {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  height: 100%;
  gap: 12px;
  padding: 8px;
}
.chat-window {
  flex: 1;
  background: #ffffff;
  border-radius: 12px;
  padding: 15px;
  overflow-y: auto;
  color: #14532d;
  font-size: clamp(13px, 1.1vw, 17px);
}
.message { margin-bottom: 10px; line-height: 1.5; }
.user-message strong { color: #38bdf8; }
.assistant-message strong { color: #a78bfa; }
.chat-input-row {
  display: flex;
  gap: 10px;
}
.chat-input-row input {
  flex: 1;
  padding: 10px 14px;
  border-radius: 8px;
  border: 1px solid #bbf7d0;
  background: #f0fdf4;
  color: #14532d;
  font-size: clamp(13px, 1.1vw, 17px);
  outline: none;
}
.chat-input-row button {
  padding: 10px 22px;
  border-radius: 8px;
  border: none;
  background: #16a34a;
  color: #ffffff;
  font-weight: bold;
  cursor: pointer;
  font-size: clamp(13px, 1.1vw, 17px);
}
.chat-input-row button:hover { background: #7dd3fc; }
</style>

<div class="chat-shell">
  <div class="chat-window" id="chat-window">
  </div>
  <div class="chat-input-row">
    <input id="chat-input" type="text" placeholder="Ask about fraud risk patterns..." />
    <button id="chat-send" type="button">Send</button>
  </div>
</div>
<script src="scripts/chat.js"></script>
""")
```
